title: Level 461

{{Background|file=Forest461.jpg}}
<div style="font-family:Georgia">
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 461: "Still Mountain State Park"}}{{Class 1e}}
Level 461, known as "Still Mountain State Park", is the 462nd level of the Backrooms.

== Description ==
Level 461, also known as "Still Mountain State Park", takes the form of a vast, fog-blanketed forest in the middle of a pristine, abandoned state park atop a mountain. The level is divided into 5 main areas: the cabins, the trails, the tennis courts, the campground, and the exterior. All sections except the exterior are on top of the mountain. Generally speaking, the trails are closer to the edge of the mountain, with most of the cabins (as well as the visitors' center) not far from (or overlooking) the rim, either. The tennis courts, the campground, and most park amenities are closer to the center of the park. The temperature in the park consistently fluctuates between 50-55 °F in the daytime and 40-45 °F at night.

The forest is mostly evergreen, primarily pine and cedar, with occasional deciduous trees such as oak or maple. There is little ground vegetation in the forest, mainly consisting of small, flimsy saplings, tufts of grass, and weeds. The full-grown trees have proved extremely difficult to cut down, even with equipment like chainsaws. There have been no reports of seeing any tree fall naturally, despite fallen trees having been found on various occasions. Through the forest wind several paved one-lane roads and one two-lane road, which, along with trails, serve as the primary methods of transit within the park. Locations of interest are often separated by as much as half a mile or more of road covered with fog thick enough to obscure vision beyond 100 feet or so, which has led some wanderers to believe that they are walking down an endless road and getting nowhere. However, it has been documented that these roads do possess Euclidean geometry; continuing down the path will eventually lead one to their destination.

The phenomenon known as "'''Alone'''" is present in Level 461; however, this particular version of the isolating property is unique in that it can be negated through use of "Group Campsite #4" (see Tennis Courts below).

*
<tabber>
|-| Trails=
Level 461 has about a dozen trails that wind throughout the park, typically along or near the park's rim, although there are some that go through the center. The trails vary in difficulty from "Easy" to "Strenuous", according to signs located near trailheads. Most trails are marked by diamond-shaped blazes nailed to trees at regular intervals along the trail. Most trails aren't hard to hike, and the only real danger they offer is sharp drop-offs on the side, as well as (after rain) slick rocks.
=== The Rim Trail ===
The Rim Trail is the primary trail in the park, and connects to most other trails. It circles the rim of the mountain, and is divided into three main sections of roughly equal length. The trail is a loop with no true start or end, but there are trailheads at three points around the mountain, specifically the visitors' center, the waterfall, and Sunrise Point. The trailheads also serve as dividers of the three sections. The trail is marked 'easy' from Sunrise Point to the waterfall, 'moderate' from the waterfall to the visitors' center and 'strenuous' from there to Sunrise Point. It's wide enough in most places for one person, two in the easy stretch. It also passes Sunset Point between the waterfall and the visitors' center. It has yellow blazes.
=== The Bench Trail ===
Still Mountain has a second flat stretch about one-third of the way down the side of the mountain, referred to as the 'bench'. The Bench Trail is a very wide and easy trail that goes around the 'bench' of the mountain. The trail is blazed blue. It is typically accessed via trails that connect to the Rim Trail. Several "Group Campsites", open clearings on the side of the trail, are present in clumps of 2-3 in intervals around the mountain. The Rim Trail and Bench Trail are the two main trails.
=== Other Trails ===
Other trails found in the park include:
{{Blurimage|caption=The Spring Steps Trailhead. This photograph was found in Level 416.|file=The Spring Steps Trailhead.png|align=right}}
* The Spring Steps Trail: Get on the Rim Trail at the VIC, walking towards Sunrise Point. The first 'strenuous' sign you come across marks the Spring Steps trail on your left. The trail consists of about 500 steps going straight down to the Bench Trail. It then dips below the Bench Trail, passes a natural spring, and ends by rejoining the Bench Trail. The trail is blazed white.
* The Summit Pavilion Trail: Found on the south side of the mountain. It forms a rectangle, with the Rim and Bench Trails as the long sides (although it never actually joins the Bench Trail, it just follows it) and two switchback-filled short sides all its own. It passes by Fern Lake, where one can switch to the Bench Trail if they wish. The trail is blazed red.
* The Mint Springs Trail: The trailhead intersects the Rim Trail, and has a parking lot nearby. The trail connects the Rim and Bench. It's steep and passes through boulder fields and behind a waterfall with very slight flow. The plants growing next to the trail smell very minty. The trail is blazed green.
* The Varnall Springs Trail: A trail that branches off from the bench trail and leads downhill for about half a mile before dead-ending at a small, iron-covered stone spring. The trail is blazed orange.

|-| Cabins=
There are many cabins at the state park, though only some are accessible. The cabins are typically located around the outer rim of the mountain, close enough that one can get on the Rim Trail from the cabins' backyards. A few are located a bit closer to the center. The cabins are numbered 60-75, despite there not being that many cabins in the park. The walls and windows of all cabins are indestructible<ref>Except by the tornado during the Clear Skies event.</ref>, and usually appear to be constructed from wood and glass, respectively. Some cabins are locked with digital padlocks. These can be opened with the last 4 digits of the wanderer's phone number. (In the "communal version" [see Tennis Courts], or if the wanderer has no phone, the passcode is 0000.) A few cabins are locked without padlocks; these cannot be entered by any known means. Most cabins have relatively few amenities, typically including a bed, a bathroom, couches, and sometimes a day or two's worth of food in fridges and/or pantries. The two major exceptions to this rule are Cabin 74 and the Wilson Cabin.

==== Cabin 74 ====
Cabin 74 is an A-frame cabin located about 500 feet from the Rim Trail. Its passcode is always "1234" regardless of the wanderer's phone number. The cabin contains a spacious living room with a TV on a wall and, curiously, a Christmas tree in one corner. The cabin is stocked with a four-day supply of hearty comfort food, including meats, fruits, breads, and vegetables, as well as Almond Water. The food can be reheated in the kitchen microwave in 30 seconds and still taste as if it had just been freshly cooked. The cabin also has a jacuzzi tub in its main bathroom. Running the jacuzzi gives the water a sweet, relaxing smell.

==== Wilson Cabin ====
The Wilson Cabin is a small house-like cabin located along the rim of the mountain, overlooking the Rim Trail. Its passcode is "3999". The cabin does not appear to be an official state park cabin, instead being a privately operated vacation house. The cabin is a small, cozy, well-stocked affair. It has a large screened back porch that overlooks a spacious backyard and the Rim Trail. The cabinets are stocked with sufficient food and water (including Almond Water) for about 10 days. The cabin has two bedrooms (one with a king-size bed and the other with two twin beds) and two full bathrooms, as well as a kitchen/dining room and a living room. The living room has a TV, a DVD player with about 2 dozen DVDs, several old children's books, glass windows overlooking the foggy backyard and an exit to the screen porch. Similarly to Cabin 74, the microwave can be used to fresh-cook any food in 30 seconds.
|-| Campground=
At the center of the park is a large campground. The campsites are mostly empty. They are connected by an indefinitely long twisting gravel road with turnoffs into each campsite. Each site has a picnic table and a fire ring, as well as a trash can and a lantern post. There are posts at the entrance of each campsite with rotatable signs on top that say "AVAILABLE" on a green background on one side and "RESERVED" on a red background on the other. Most campsites (~80%) are "reserved". A few sites (~10%) have empty RVs or tents in them, or smoldering ashes in the fire rings.

The fog is thicker than elsewhere in the campground, which may have a possible connection to the campground's non-Euclidean geometry. After wandering down the curvy road for 100 campsites, wanderers will come across a fork in the road. A sign shows one path leading to sites 101-200, and the other leading to the playground, which is located next to the campground entrance. Following the path to other campsites will lead deeper into the campground, past 100 more campsites, and eventually to a similar fork leading to either sites 201-300 or the playground. It is not known if there is a limit to how far one can go into the campground. Taking the road back to the playground will lead there shortly. Attempting to double back after reaching the playground will lead to sites 1-100 again, regardless of how many sites deep one was when they returned to the playground. Wandering away from the campground will lead to a stretch of forest and eventually back to the previous 100-site segment, or, if one was in sites 1-100, back to the playground.

The following sites display mild anomalies compared to the rest of the campground:

* Sites 3, 9, 19, 27, 50, 81, 122 and 243 are <u>always</u> marked "AVAILABLE", and the signs cannot be rotated.
* Sites 6, 24, 39, 59, 73, 99, 101 and 398 are <u>always</u> marked "RESERVED", and the signs cannot be rotated.
* Sites 12, 66 and 304 always have glowing embers in the fire ring.
* Site 150 has a canopy set up over the picnic table.
* Site 399 has a small cooler on the picnic table containing refrigerated lunch meat, fruit and potato chips.
* Site 640 has 3 40-pound barbells next to the picnic table.
* Site 999 has building blocks and an umbrella on the picnic table.
|-| Tennis Courts=
At the point where the loop containing the Taylor Cabin connects to the road leading to the VIC and campground, there is a pair of tennis courts. The courts are paved with black asphalt. The asphalt on the back side of the court on the right is cracked in a few places, causing balls hit on the cracks to bounce in random directions. The courts have four light poles, one at each court end, that come on at night, providing better visibility. Eight rackets and a mesh bag of 15 tennis balls can be found on a metal bench by the right court's net.

Behind the courts is a small, open field. In the far corner of the field is a baseball backstop, though there is no other baseball equipment in the field. In the near corner is an area marked "Group Campsite #4". There is a sign, similar to those seen in the campground, marked "RESERVED". Turning this sign to "AVAILABLE" will cause one to instantly and seamlessly enter the "communal version" of Level 461. This is a version in which Phenomenon 15 does not apply, allowing one to interact with other wanderers in the communal version. Turning the sign back will not affect the sign in the communal version but instead return the wanderer to their personal version of Level 461. It is possible to take others back to your version of the level provided that they are in physical contact with you when you turn the sign.
|-| Exterior=
It is not known what lies beyond the mountain. When the fog clears on certain Tuesdays (see below), the views around the mountain reveal a vast, hilly forest patched with what appears to be farmland and power lines. The forest continues to the horizon, where hazy yet distinct shapes of other mountains can be seen. A river can also be seen winding through the forest, and on rare occasions one may see a plume of smoke issuing from an unknown source.

The mountain, as far as is known, can only be descended by means of a road near the campground that winds down the mountain in a series of switchbacks. If the mountain is descended on any foggy day, the switchbacks appear to continue descending indefinitely, and turning around will bring one back to the top in two or three switchbacks. If one attempts to leave the road, they will invariably wind up back in the forest atop the mountain in a random location in the park. If the mountain is descended during the Clear Skies event, the bottom can be reached after 12-15 switchbacks, and the road will straighten out and head away from the mountain. However, it has been observed that a noticeable proportion of trees off the are painted with purple blazes, a common "no trespassing" symbol. Additionally, wanderers have reported a sense of paranoia and being watched while off the mountain. This feeling always fades completely away once one reascends the mountain. Every time wanderers remained off the mountain until the fog returned, contact was lost with the wanderer and they ended up missing. All such wanderers are presumed dead. It is believed that this is due to the lightning from the storms that occur during the Clear Skies event.
</tabber>


=== Weather ===
An anomaly in Level 461 causes the weather in the level to follow a predictable 7-day cycle. Note that the weather is foggy every day except Tuesday.

* Monday: Still in the morning, still and rainy in the afternoon and night.
* Tuesday: Light rain and breeze in the morning, still in the afternoon and night. (25%: Clear Skies event instead (see below).)
* Wednesday: Still all day, breezy and rainy all night.
* Thursday: Breeze in the morning, still in the afternoon and night.
* Friday: Still in the morning, light rain and breeze in the afternoon, rainy all night.
* Saturday: Rainy in the morning; every other week rainy in afternoon and night as well.
* Sunday: Still all day and night.

==== Clear Skies Event ====
The Clear Skies event is a weather event that occurs on a few random Tuesdays. It is the primary danger of Level 461.

The Clear Skies event has a 25% chance of occurring each week. It begins on Tuesday morning, when the fog slowly dissipates, resulting in a sunny day instead of Tuesday's normal windy, rainy fog. The sunshine persists all morning and afternoon long, and the temperature will rise to between 65-70 °F. During this time, birdsong may be heard in the trees, though as very few birds have been seen in this level, the sound's true source is unknown. All geometry in the level becomes fully Euclidean during this time, and as a result, the campground's campsites (other than 1-100) cannot be accessed during this time.<ref>If the wanderer is in campground sites 101+ when the Clear Skies event begins, the fog will not lift and they will be unable to leave the campground until the fog returns at 7 PM. However, several sites will fill with coolers containing food and supplies such as Almond Water.</ref> During the afternoon, the sky will slowly become partly cloudy, and the sun will go behind the clouds and come back out dozens of times. The sky will gradually become cloudier and cloudier, until it is completely overcast by 6 PM. One should return to shelter, preferably in either the Wilson Cabin or the visitors' center, before night completely falls.

As the sun sets (which is visible through a gap in the clouds at Sunset Point), the fog will reappear in the valley and rise back up the mountain until it is completely foggy again by 7 PM. At this point, the wind will begin to pick up, and thunder will be audible in the distance. Around 7:30, rain will begin to fall, and the wind will start to blow harder, with the strength of both the wind and the rain, as well as the thunder, gradually increasing until a full-strength thunderstorm is in progress by 8 PM. Lightning strikes are intense, close-by and frequent, and if one is outside one should stay as low as possible and avoid puddles or streams of water if possible. Brief bouts of hail may also occur between 8 PM and 9 PM. Around 9 PM, a roaring sound will become audible, especially outside, and any radios and/or TVs that are on in the cabins will give a tornado warning. The tornado has been confirmed to be real, and while it does not seem to target wanderers, it is as dangerous as a real-life tornado, including flying debris. This state of weather will last until around 10 PM, when the roaring sound will fade into the normal noise of the thunderstorm. However, the wind, rain and lightning will have decreased noticeably in intensity by this point, and will gradually fade until 11 PM, when the wind and lightning will have ceased entirely, leaving only a moderate, steady drizzle. The rain will stop between 1-2 AM, and by morning the level's weather schedule will have returned to normal.

In most cases, one can survive the Clear Skies event by taking shelter, but there have been instances of shelters getting hit by the tornado. It has been reported that a few wanderers have survived the destruction of the cabins. Oddly enough, the visitors' center and the Wilson Cabin (as far as is known) have never been destroyed by the tornado; it is unknown whether this is due to random chance or whether the VIC and Wilson Cabin are anomalous safe spaces one can use to guarantee survival. Regardless, it is advised to remain in one of these two buildings during the Clear Skies event at night.

== Entities ==
Although the forest gives all the indications of hosting a thriving ecosystem, actually seeing wildlife other than deer is relatively rare. It is not known whether these are true Frontrooms deer or simply entities resembling deer that are native to the level.

== Colonies and Outposts ==
=== Camp 461 ===

* A small group located in the "communal" version of Level 461.
* Lives in Group Campsite #4, in the main campground and in the Wilson Cabin.
* Scavenges the Campground for resources.
* ~50 members; willing to trade.
* Accepting new members.

== Entrances and Exits ==

=== Entrances ===

* On [[Level 416]], occasional signs can be found next to turnoffs from the main boardwalk. They read "Still Mountain State Park -- X miles", where X has been reported to be anywhere from 3 to 15. Following the turnoff for the specified number of miles will lead to a dead end in the trail. Going beyond the dead end into the forest will result in one winding up in the campground.
* No-clipping into a similar sign on [[Level 427]] will lead here.
* Straight paths of missing trees on [[Level 393]] may lead here.

=== Exits ===

* The emergency exit at the rear of the visitors' center leads to [[Level 462]].
* On Tuesdays (Clear Skies), it instead leads to [[Level 416]] (67%), or [[Level 83]] (33%).
* Running both showers at once in the Wilson Cabin will transport one to [[Level 37]].
* Touching the spring at the foot of the Varnall Springs trail at night during the Clear Skies event will transport one to [[Level 630]].
* Finding a rare house lit up with Halloween decorations and going inside leads to [[Level 9.31]]. A similar house with Christmas decorations leads to [[Level 633]].
